verses.text: All Israel was reckoned in the genealogies, then carried to Babylon 'for their transgression'; now the first to return and resettle are named. The exile is owned as judgment, the return as grace. God both judges sin and restores a remnant.

All Israel was reckoned in the genealogies, then carried to Babylon 'for their transgression'; now the first to return and resettle are named. The exile is owned as judgment, the return as grace. God both judges sin and restores a remnant.

All Israel was reckoned in the genealogies, then carried to Babylon 'for their transgression'; now the first to return and resettle are named. The exile is owned as judgment, the return as grace. God both judges sin and restores a remnant.
Families of Judah who resettled in Jerusalem are listed. The chosen tribe came home to the holy city. God brought His people back to the land their fathers had lost, replanting them by grace.
Families of Judah who resettled in Jerusalem are listed. The chosen tribe came home to the holy city. God brought His people back to the land their fathers had lost, replanting them by grace.
Families of Judah who resettled in Jerusalem are listed. The chosen tribe came home to the holy city. God brought His people back to the land their fathers had lost, replanting them by grace.

Benjamin's returning families are recorded, chief men of their fathers. Alongside Judah, Benjamin repopulated Jerusalem. The remnant that came home was the living continuation of the ancient covenant people.

Benjamin's returning families are recorded, chief men of their fathers. Alongside Judah, Benjamin repopulated Jerusalem. The remnant that came home was the living continuation of the ancient covenant people.

Benjamin's returning families are recorded, chief men of their fathers. Alongside Judah, Benjamin repopulated Jerusalem. The remnant that came home was the living continuation of the ancient covenant people.

The priests who returned are named, 'very able men for the work of the service of the house of God.' The restored community gathered first around the temple. God preserved able priests to lead His people in worship again.

The priests who returned are named, 'very able men for the work of the service of the house of God.' The restored community gathered first around the temple. God preserved able priests to lead His people in worship again.

The priests who returned are named, 'very able men for the work of the service of the house of God.' The restored community gathered first around the temple. God preserved able priests to lead His people in worship again.

The priests who returned are named, 'very able men for the work of the service of the house of God.' The restored community gathered first around the temple. God preserved able priests to lead His people in worship again.

The Levites who returned are listed, descendants of the temple singers and servants. The ministry of God's house was restored with its families. He kept alive those who would lead His praise after the exile.

The Levites who returned are listed, descendants of the temple singers and servants. The ministry of God's house was restored with its families. He kept alive those who would lead His praise after the exile.

The Levites who returned are listed, descendants of the temple singers and servants. The ministry of God's house was restored with its families. He kept alive those who would lead His praise after the exile.

The gatekeepers are recorded, an office traced to faithful Phinehas and ordained by David and Samuel. Guarding the house of God was an honoured trust. Even the keepers of the doors served a holy and necessary work.

The gatekeepers are recorded, an office traced to faithful Phinehas and ordained by David and Samuel. Guarding the house of God was an honoured trust. Even the keepers of the doors served a holy and necessary work.

The gatekeepers are recorded, an office traced to faithful Phinehas and ordained by David and Samuel. Guarding the house of God was an honoured trust. Even the keepers of the doors served a holy and necessary work.

Two hundred twelve porters were set in their office by their genealogy, stationed at the four sides. The guarding of God's house was carefully ordered. Faithfulness at every gate kept the holy place secure.

Two hundred twelve porters were set in their office by their genealogy, stationed at the four sides. The guarding of God's house was carefully ordered. Faithfulness at every gate kept the holy place secure.

Two hundred twelve porters were set in their office by their genealogy, stationed at the four sides. The guarding of God's house was carefully ordered. Faithfulness at every gate kept the holy place secure.

The chief porters lodged around the house, for the charge was on them, and the morning opening pertained to them. They bore a daily, reliable trust. To open God's house each morning was a sacred, faithful duty.

The chief porters lodged around the house, for the charge was on them, and the morning opening pertained to them. They bore a daily, reliable trust. To open God's house each morning was a sacred, faithful duty.

The chief porters lodged around the house, for the charge was on them, and the morning opening pertained to them. They bore a daily, reliable trust. To open God's house each morning was a sacred, faithful duty.

Certain Levites had charge of the vessels, the fine flour, the wine, oil, and spices. Every detail of the temple's service had its keeper. God's worship was conducted with order and faithful care down to the smallest thing.

Certain Levites had charge of the vessels, the fine flour, the wine, oil, and spices. Every detail of the temple's service had its keeper. God's worship was conducted with order and faithful care down to the smallest thing.

Certain Levites had charge of the vessels, the fine flour, the wine, oil, and spices. Every detail of the temple's service had its keeper. God's worship was conducted with order and faithful care down to the smallest thing.

Others prepared the shewbread, and the singers were free of other duties, employed in their work day and night. Continual praise had its appointed ministers. The house of God was filled with ordered, unceasing service.

Others prepared the shewbread, and the singers were free of other duties, employed in their work day and night. Continual praise had its appointed ministers. The house of God was filled with ordered, unceasing service.

Others prepared the shewbread, and the singers were free of other duties, employed in their work day and night. Continual praise had its appointed ministers. The house of God was filled with ordered, unceasing service.
The family at Gibeon, ancestors of Saul, is recorded again. The genealogy circles back to the king's line. The Chronicler prepares to turn from the lists to the story of Israel's first king.

The family at Gibeon, ancestors of Saul, is recorded again. The genealogy circles back to the king's line. The Chronicler prepares to turn from the lists to the story of Israel's first king.

Saul's line is named once more — Ner, Kish, Saul, and Jonathan with his sons. The king's house appears again at the genealogy's close. God preserves the record even of the family whose throne was taken away.

Saul's line is named once more — Ner, Kish, Saul, and Jonathan with his sons. The king's house appears again at the genealogy's close. God preserves the record even of the family whose throne was taken away.

Saul's line is named once more — Ner, Kish, Saul, and Jonathan with his sons. The king's house appears again at the genealogy's close. God preserves the record even of the family whose throne was taken away.

Jonathan's descendants are followed for several generations to Azel and his sons. The line of David's beloved friend lived on. Having named the people, the Chronicler now turns from lists to the history that opens with Saul's fall.
